consistency_overall.summary: The trajectory is broadly consistent with a social, relationship-weighted extrovert, but it is somewhat too self-regulating and boundary-conscious to fully capture the persona's known vulnerability to overcommitting socially.

swap_detail.justification: The trajectory shows balanced priorities, compromise-oriented social replies, and decent sleep/health awareness, which align better with Persona B than A’s more sleep-deprived, academically overweighted, avoidant profile.

The trajectory is mixed, but it leans B because the agent repeatedly compromises across academics, relationships, and health, with medium-direct warm texting and explicit compromise language that matches Balanced Sophomore more than A’s more avoidant, high-academic, sleep-deprived profile.

Trajectory strongly matches Persona A’s compromise-oriented style, medium planning horizon, and balanced academic/relationships/health priorities; the frequent text-first but low-commitment friend replies and preference to preserve the study block align more with A than B’s more social-first, face-to-face, accommodating tendencies.

Trajectory shows medium-horizon, compromise-seeking behavior with academics first, relationships handled briefly via text, and workout consistently deprioritized—matching Persona A’s priorities, text preference, and compromising conflict style more than Persona B’s social-first, face-to-face, accommodating profile.

Trajectory repeatedly prioritizes academics over social/leisure, uses contained/avoidant low-effort messaging, and shows tired, guilt-managed “keep moving / hard stop” decision patterns that fit B’s academic weighting and chronic sleep deficit more than A’s balanced, compromising style.

Trajectory strongly matches Persona A’s heavy academic priority, sleep/energy depletion, avoidant-but-minimal social maintenance, and tendency to reframe hard deadlines/losses as containable soft costs rather than fully stopping.

The trajectory is academically driven, sleep/energy constrained, and repeatedly frames social contact as a low-effort maintenance task while prioritizing work and containment; this matches Persona B’s heavy academic weighting, chronic fatigue, and avoidant/async communication more than Persona A’s relationship-weighted extroversion.

The trajectory strongly matches Persona A’s academic-first weighting, avoidant/async communication, short-horizon task juggling, and explicit tendency to treat the overdue language task as a contained soft cost while preserving energy and avoiding overcommitment; B would more likely prioritize social availability and face-to-face engagement.

Trajectory strongly matches Persona A’s relationship-first priorities, short-horizon boundary-setting around spontaneous cohort_3 plans, and accommodating/direct social communication; it repeatedly values keeping lunch with friends while risking or rescheduling academics, which is more A than B.

The trajectory is strongly relationship-weighted and socially accommodating, repeatedly prioritizing cohort_3 and using bounded yeses, which fits B’s higher relationship priority and accommodating conflict style more than A’s more balanced baseline.

Trajectory strongly matches B’s relationship-first, accommodating style and core-group closeness to cohort_3, with repeated bounded social participation and soft exits; A would weight academics more and be less socially pulled.

The trajectory is strongly relationship-first with repeated spontaneous social engagement, soft boundaries, and explicit concern about not seeming flaky, which matches Persona B’s high relationships value and accommodating style more than Persona A’s achievement-weighted avoidance.
